chief_complaint: coughing up blood twice this morning
hpi_details: two episodes of haemoptysis — approximately 2 tablespoons each time, bright red blood mixed with sputum, associated mild right-sided pleuritic chest pain, 3-week history of dry cough and fever, 4kg weight loss over 2 months, night sweats
ideas: she recently moved from the Philippines, thinks maybe it's a bad infection from the plane
concerns: very frightened, never coughed blood before, worried about TB because her uncle had it
expectations: wants to know what's wrong urgently and if she needs to be isolated
pmh: asthma as a child, nil current medical conditions
medications: salbutamol PRN (rarely uses), oral contraceptive pill
social_hx: non-smoker, non-drinker, immigrated from Philippines 6 months ago, healthcare worker (clinic assistant), lives with 4 family members

reference_soap.subjective: 29F Filipino healthcare worker presenting with x2 episodes haemoptysis this morning, right pleuritic chest pain, 3-week cough and fever, 4kg weight loss, night sweats. Family history of TB. Lives in shared accommodation.
reference_soap.objective: O2 saturation, CXR (apical shadowing, cavitation?), sputum AFB x3, Mantoux/IGRA, HIV test, FBC, CRP. ISOLATE pending results.
reference_soap.assessment: Pulmonary tuberculosis until proven otherwise. DDx: community acquired pneumonia, bronchiectasis, PE with pulmonary infarction, lung malignancy (less likely at 29).
reference_soap.plan: Respiratory isolation, notify public health, sputum AFB x3 on consecutive days, CXR, IGRA, HIV counselling and testing, contact tracing (4 household contacts + workplace), RIPE therapy if confirmed.

learning_objectives: Elicit TB risk factors systematically: immigration, contacts, occupation, HIV status
Handle haemoptysis history sensitively in a frightened patient
Explain isolation and public health notification appropriately
Demonstrate cultural sensitivity with an immigrant patient
